figures.caption: Figure 8: Visual examples of the 20 image degradations considered in this work. Different degradations affect semantic entities, local structure, and global visual content in different ways.

Figure 12: Examples of image pairs with relation-level semantic changes. In each pair, the main semantic entities are largely preserved, while the relations among them are changed. The first three pairs, (a)–(c), are real photographs, and the last two pairs, (d) and (e), are generated examples. The corresponding T3S scores are: (a) 0.7641, (b) 0.8062, (c) 0.8231, (d) 0.8316, and (e) 0.8293.

Figure 1: How to evaluate Semantic Similarity? Existing IQA methods cannot fully capture semantic preservation. In particular, generative restoration may introduce semantic distortions, which motivates Semantic similarity as a new task in low-level image processing.
Figure 5: Comparison of score curves of different methods on the COCO dataset under four low-level image processing tasks.
Figure 6: Comparison of score curves of different methods on the SPA-Data dataset under four low-level image processing tasks.

Figure 11: Case study on entity-level semantic changes. Each group contains three comparison levels: Level 1 applies strong snow degradation to the original image, Level 2 replaces key entities with semantically related ones, and Level 3 replaces key entities with semantically unrelated ones.

Figure 2: General formulation of semantic similarity based on pairwise weighted matching. The size of each entity denotes its semantic importance in computing 𝜌𝑖, 𝑗 , while the relation links denote relation importance in computing 𝜌𝑖𝑘,𝑗𝑙 .

Figure 7: This figure summarizes our evaluation protocol under different levels of semantic change. To assess the proposed method under varying degrees of semantic change, we construct multiple levels of semantic variation through image processing and perform experiments accordingly.

Figure 4: The FBDmodule performs feature disentanglement via attraction and repulsion. It pulls semantically consistent features together and pushes inconsistent ones apart, thereby enhancing the distinction between foreground and background entities.

Figure 3: Overview of the proposed T3S framework. Given an input image pair, SAM first extracts SEs and decouples them into foreground and background to model the triplet components 𝜖F and 𝜖B. Meanwhile, Qwen2.5-VL predicts class and relation labels from foreground entities to model the relational component 𝜖R. The final T3S index is obtained by coupling the triplet (𝜖F, 𝜖B, 𝜖R).
